title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Automation and Data Client Systems · Language Clinic: Freshness Age
summary: A public-data mirror uses validators and local metadata but has confused stale files, 304 metadata, Vary, and authenticated responses. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py freshness age increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers spec-aware cache decision notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compute freshness from explicit directives, dates, age, and response context rather than wall-clock intuition. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
